message:
fwc: robust workspace-member detection, strict manifest fallback, scaffolded e2e harness

Three independent fwc changes, grouped because they all tighten the
scaffolding path to produce correct, compilable connectors instead of
silently-broken ones.

new_cmd.rs — workspace-member detection
  update_workspace_members() used a simple substring match on
  "\"path\"" against the entire Cargo.toml.  That matched path strings
  embedded anywhere in the file — other tables, comments, or nested
  arrays — so the scaffolder would sometimes believe a workspace
  already contained a member it did not, and silently skip inserting
  the new entry.  Replaced with workspace_members_contains(), which
  walks the file a section at a time and only considers lines inside
  [workspace] that either start with "members" or are quoted array
  entries.  This is still string-based (full TOML parsing/round-trip
  would reflow the file), but the scope is now unambiguous.

new_cmd.rs — generated e2e tests
  The scaffolded e2e tests imported crate::error::* via \`use
  crate::error::{struct_name}Error\`.  That fails when the scaffold is
  consumed as a dependency because crate:: resolves to the generator
  crate, not the generated connector crate.  Every such import now uses
  the \`{crate_ident}::error::\` path template, which the scaffolder
  substitutes per-connector.  The generated e2e harness also gains a
  proper SDK-backed helper set (signing key, capability token factories,
  handshake/invoke/simulate builders) so the bundled tests actually
  speak the connector protocol instead of raw JSON-RPC.

readiness.rs — strict [provides.operations] requirement
  discovered_connector_from_toml() used to fall back to an empty ops
  map when [provides.operations] was missing.  That masked a real
  manifest defect: a connector claiming to be loadable with no
  advertised operations is broken, but the rest of the pipeline would
  treat it as a valid empty connector.  We now .with_context() and
  propagate the error so \`fwc\` readiness checks surface the missing
  table explicitly.  Covered by the new
  toml_fallback_requires_provides_operations_table test.

files.patch:
@@ -2494,12 +2494,16 @@ fn discovered_connector_from_toml(
         .strip_prefix("fcp.")
         .unwrap_or(connector_id.as_str())
         .to_owned();
-    let empty_ops = toml::map::Map::new();
     let operations_table = document
         .get("provides")
         .and_then(|provides| provides.get("operations"))
         .and_then(toml::Value::as_table)
-        .unwrap_or(&empty_ops);
+        .with_context(|| {
+            format!(
+                "{} is missing [provides.operations]",
+                manifest_path.display()
+            )
+        })?;
     let mut operations = operations_table
         .iter()
         .map(|(operation_id, operation)| {
@@ -7917,6 +7921,57 @@ deny_ptrace = true
         );
     }
 
+    #[test]
+    fn toml_fallback_requires_provides_operations_table() {
+        let document: toml::Value = toml::from_str(
+            r#"
+[connector]
+id = "fcp.truthful-missing-ops"
+name = "Truthful Missing Ops"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "Fallback manifest missing operations"
+format = "wasi"
+
+[zones]
+home = "z:work"
+allowed_sources = ["z:work"]
+allowed_targets = ["z:work"]
+forbidden = []
+
+[capabilities]
+required = []
+optional = []
+forbidden = []
+
+[sandbox]
+profile = "strict"
+memory_mb = 64
+cpu_percent = 20
+wall_clock_timeout_ms = 1000
+fs_readonly_paths = ["/usr"]
+fs_writable_paths = ["$CONNECTOR_STATE"]
+deny_exec = true
+deny_ptrace = true
+"#,
+        )
+        .expect("fallback document should parse");
+
+        let error = discovered_connector_from_toml(
+            "truthful-missing-ops",
+            Path::new("connectors/truthful-missing-ops/manifest.toml"),
+            &document,
+            "strict manifest parsing failed",
+        )
+        .expect_err("discovery should fail without [provides.operations]");
+
+        assert!(
+            error
+                .to_string()
+                .contains("missing [provides.operations]"),
+            "unexpected error: {error:?}"
+        );
+    }
+
     #[test]
     fn discovered_operation_from_toml_does_not_claim_simulate_support() {
         let manifest: toml::Value = toml::from_str(
